title: agrostis reinwardtii var. borneensis
intro: Agrostis reinwardtii var. borneensis, also known as Borneo bent grass, is a type of grass native to the island of Borneo in Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, Borneo bent grass has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Borneo bent grass can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which may be used to alleviate various health conditions.
